```python
class Solution:
    def maxLengthBetweenEqualCharacters(self, s: str) -> int:
        max_length = -1
        for char in set(s):
            indices = [i for i, x in enumerate(s) if x == char]
            if len(indices) > 1:
                max_length = max(max_length, max(indices) - min(indices) - 1)
        return max_length
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution iterates over each unique character in the string, finds all its indices, and calculates the maximum length of the substring between the first and last occurrence of the character, excluding the characters themselves.
